title: crepis foetida subsp. vulgaris
intro: Crepis foetida subsp. vulgaris, also known as the stinking hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family Asteraceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which provide its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Crepis foetida subsp. vulgaris can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
